series.title: Contract Bride
series.tagline: She signed for money. She stayed for revenge.

scenes.caption: The Signature
She was wrong.
One call changes it all
To be continued...
Strangers at the Altar
She was wrong.
One call changes it all
To be continued...
Welcome to your new prison.
Rule one: never touch me.
He thinks he owns me.
She wasn't supposed to fight back.
Every rule protects a secret.
He knew her. He lied.
She didn't marry for money.
Now he knows she knows.
She's back. And she's not asking permission.
scenes.narration: The mansion doors swing open, and she walks in like she owns everything—including my husband's past.

scenes.caption: He turns pale. Who is she to him?
scenes.narration: Elena, my ice-cold billionaire husband, freezes at the sight of her. In two years, I've never seen him afraid.

scenes.caption: I'm the wife. So why do I feel like the intruder?
scenes.narration: I stand in my simple silk gown, invisible, as the woman glides toward him. 'Adrian, darling, did you miss me?'

scenes.caption: The woman who broke him first.
scenes.narration: 'Vivian,' Adrian whispers, his voice cracking. The name he swore he'd buried. His first love. The one who left him at the altar.

scenes.caption: She knows about the contract.
scenes.narration: Vivian's eyes slice toward me. 'So this is the little charity case you married for the company?' She laughs. 'How much did he pay you, sweetheart?'

scenes.caption: Someone betrayed me.
scenes.narration: My blood runs cold. Only three people knew the truth of our marriage. How does she know?

scenes.caption: She came armed with paper.
scenes.narration: Adrian steps between us, jaw tight. 'Vivian, you have no right to be here.' But she smiles, pulling a folded document from her purse.

scenes.caption: Still married... to her?!
scenes.narration: She unfolds it slowly. 'Actually, darling—I have every right. Because legally, I'm still your wife.' The room spins as Adrian's face shatters.

scenes.caption: The world is watching us tonight.
scenes.narration: The paparazzi swarm the charity gala, and the contract demands one thing: proof of love.

scenes.caption: Smile. Convince them we're in love.
scenes.narration: Damian pulls her close, his whisper as sharp as ice. Smile, wife. Convince them.

scenes.caption: Just a kiss for the cameras.
scenes.narration: His lips meet hers for the cameras — but her heart betrays the lie, racing against her will.

scenes.caption: His ex-lover won't let go.
scenes.narration: Across the room, Vivienne watches, her painted smile twisting into a blade of jealousy.

scenes.caption: That wasn't part of the deal.
scenes.narration: When they part, Elena sees it in his eyes — something real flickering beneath the ice.

scenes.caption: I can't forget why I'm here.
scenes.narration: But revenge is a promise she made in blood, and love was never on her list.

scenes.caption: I know what you really are.
scenes.narration: Vivienne approaches with a poisoned toast and a secret that could shatter everything.

scenes.caption: He's planning to destroy you.
scenes.narration: Then Vivienne leans in and whispers the truth — Damian signed a second contract, one that ends with Elena gone.

scenes.caption: A secret hidden for years...
scenes.narration: The old mansion smells of dust and secrets. Elena clutches a yellowed envelope she found hidden behind the library shelf.

scenes.caption: The hidden will changes everything.
scenes.narration: Inside is a will — signed by his late father, dated one day before his mysterious death. Her hands tremble.

scenes.caption: An heir the family buried.
scenes.narration: The words burn into her: everything belongs not to Adrian — but to a child no one has ever spoken of.

scenes.caption: He wasn't supposed to see this.
scenes.narration: Footsteps echo. Adrian enters, his cold gray eyes locking onto the paper in her grasp.

scenes.caption: 'You don't know what you've done.'
scenes.narration: 'Where did you find that?' His voice is ice, but his hands shake with something like fear.

scenes.caption: She came for money. She stays for war.
scenes.narration: Elena pulls away, heart pounding. This is the weapon she needs — the revenge she signed up for.

scenes.caption: The one person nobody expected.
scenes.narration: Then a voice from the doorway freezes them both. 'That will is fake — and I can prove it.'

scenes.caption: Her secret is about to shatter everything...
scenes.narration: Elena's blood runs cold — the woman is Adrian's presumed-dead mother. And she knows Elena's real name.

scenes.caption: Midnight. The truth finally in her hands.
scenes.narration: The clock strikes twelve, and Ella clutches the hidden will against her chest, certain tonight everything changes.

scenes.caption: He knew she was coming.
scenes.narration: Adrian appears from the shadows, his steel-grey eyes colder than she has ever seen them.

scenes.caption: "Give it to me, Ella."
scenes.narration: "That will was never yours to find," he whispers, extending his hand for it.

scenes.caption: The ex was pulling every string.
scenes.narration: Then Vivian glides in, a cruel smile curving her lips, revealing she orchestrated it all.

scenes.caption: Every kiss was a lie.
scenes.narration: "He married you to bait me," Vivian laughs, and Ella's world shatters in an instant.

scenes.caption: "You used my heart as a weapon."
scenes.narration: Adrian reaches for Ella, but she slaps his hand away, the will trembling in her grasp.

scenes.caption: Someone wants him dead — tonight.
scenes.narration: Suddenly gunshots crack through the chandelier, glass rains down, and a masked intruder aims straight at Adrian.

scenes.caption: To be continued...
scenes.narration: As the trigger clicks, Ella lunges between them — and the screen cuts to black on her scream.

scenes.caption: The mask comes off tonight.
scenes.narration: Lena stands in the shattered study, betrayal still burning in her chest. But tonight, she is no longer the frightened bride he purchased.

scenes.caption: "Who are you, really?"
scenes.narration: Adrian storms in, disbelief written across his face. The wife he underestimated holds a folder that could destroy his entire empire.

scenes.caption: "I didn't marry you for money."
scenes.narration: She drops the file on the desk. Inside lies proof that his family stole everything from hers years ago.

scenes.caption: The daughter he destroyed came back.
scenes.narration: Adrian's hands tremble as he reads. The orphan girl his father ruined has returned as his own wife.

scenes.caption: "Revenge wore a wedding dress."
scenes.narration: "Every rule you gave me, I obeyed. While I mapped every secret you tried to bury." Her voice never wavers.

scenes.caption: He never expected to fall.
scenes.narration: He reaches for her, torn between fury and a love he never meant to feel. "Then why does my heart still choose you?"

scenes.caption: "Neither of you leaves alive."
scenes.narration: Suddenly the study doors slam open. His ex-fiancée Vivian stands there, a gun glinting in her manicured hand.

scenes.caption: To be continued...
scenes.narration: Vivian's eyes gleam with madness. "You both think this was your game? The real will names me." The trigger clicks.

scenes.caption: The contract that bound them is about to burn.
scenes.narration: Elena stands before the fireplace, the marriage contract trembling in her hands. Tonight, she finally understands the price of every lie Adrian told her.

scenes.caption: He never expected her to fight back.
scenes.narration: Adrian bursts through the doors, his cold gray eyes wide with panic. For the first time, the untouchable billionaire looks truly afraid.

scenes.caption: Every secret she hid was a weapon.
scenes.narration: "You bought my name, my silence, my body," Elena whispers. "But you never bought my loyalty."

scenes.caption: The chains are finally breaking.
scenes.narration: She lets the pages slip into the flames. The contract that trapped her curls into ash and smoke.

scenes.caption: The truth she never knew.
scenes.narration: "Do you know what you've just done?" Adrian grabs her wrist. "That contract was the only thing keeping you alive."

scenes.caption: Who was really pulling the strings?
scenes.narration: Elena's heart stops. The revenge she planned suddenly feels like a trap someone else set for her.

scenes.caption: A ghost from the past returns.
scenes.narration: A phone buzzes on the marble table. The screen glows with a message from the one person they both believed was dead.

scenes.caption: The debt is far from over.
scenes.narration: As the flames die, headlights cut through the windows. Someone has come to collect what the burning contract left unpaid.

scenes.caption: The contract is gone. The war begins.
scenes.narration: The ashes of their contract still smolder on the marble floor. Elena thought burning it would set her free—but freedom always costs blood.

scenes.caption: "Who are you really, Elena?"
scenes.narration: Adrian storms in, his jaw tight, betrayal written across his face. He learned her true name only yesterday—now he demands the truth.

scenes.caption: Revenge wore a wedding dress.
scenes.narration: "I married you to destroy your family," she whispers, tears glistening. "Your father killed mine. This was never about the money."

scenes.caption: "Your father's death wasn't what you think."
scenes.narration: Adrian reels—but from his coat he pulls a folded document. The hidden will. Proof that everything she believed was a lie built by others.

scenes.caption: The ex returns—armed.
scenes.narration: Then the door bursts open. Vivienne, his ex, glides in with a cruel smile and a gun trembling in her manicured hand.

scenes.caption: "Shoot me instead."
scenes.narration: "You both ruined me," Vivienne hisses. "So neither of you leaves this room alive." Adrian steps in front of Elena.

scenes.caption: He took the bullet for her.
scenes.narration: A gunshot cracks like thunder. Elena screams as Adrian collapses into her arms, crimson blooming across his white shirt.

scenes.caption: "He's not the one you should fear."
scenes.narration: As Adrian's eyes flutter shut, he presses a ring into her palm and whispers a name she never expected. Then—another figure steps from the shadows.

series.title: The Maid Who Owned the Empire
series.tagline: They hired her to clean their mansion, never knowing she secretly held the deed to everything.
plannedEpisodeTitles: The Photograph They Should Have Burned
A Toast to the Woman They Wronged
The Signature That Buys a Dynasty
She Called Me Family—Then Sold Me Out
The Boardroom Knows My Real Name
Everything You Own Was Always Mine

scenes.caption: They hired a maid. They hired their reckoning.
scenes.narration: Elena Vasquez walks through gilded gates she once called home, wearing a maid's uniform. They fired her father, buried her family name—but they never learned her face.

scenes.caption: To him, she was invisible.
scenes.narration: Inside, billionaire Marcus Thornbury barely glances at her. To him, she is furniture. He has no idea he is standing on land she legally owns.

scenes.caption: Every insult, she remembers.
scenes.narration: His cruel daughter Victoria hurls a wine glass at Elena's feet. 'Clean it, servant.' Elena kneels, smiling secretly—patience is a weapon.

scenes.caption: The deed. Everything is hers.
scenes.narration: That night, Elena unlocks a hidden briefcase in her tiny quarters. Inside: the property deed to the entire Thornbury empire, signed decades ago to her late father.

scenes.caption: He destroyed her father. She never forgot.
scenes.narration: A framed photo slips from the deed—her father shaking hands with a younger Marcus. The betrayal that stole her family whispers back to life.

scenes.caption: She's been discovered.
scenes.narration: But someone is watching. Marcus's loyal butler, Gregory, has seen the deed. He knows exactly who she is.

scenes.caption: He knows. Or does he?
scenes.narration: At breakfast, Marcus taps his newspaper. 'I hear the true owner of my estate is closer than I think.' Elena's coffee pot freezes mid-pour.

scenes.caption: One envelope could end everything.
scenes.narration: Gregory steps forward, envelope in hand. 'Sir, there's something you must see about this woman.' Elena's heart stops. Her secret hangs by a single breath.

scenes.caption: Day one. Scrubbing my enemies' floors.
scenes.narration: Dawn breaks over the Ashford mansion as Elena kneels on the marble floor, scrubbing bucket at her side. They think she is nothing. They have no idea she owns the very ground beneath them.

scenes.caption: Pick that up, girl.
scenes.narration: Victoria Ashford descends the grand staircase, dripping in diamonds, her cruel smile cutting the air. She tosses her fur coat at Elena's feet without a glance.

scenes.caption: They don't know what I'm hiding.
scenes.narration: Elena retrieves the coat with trembling hands, biting back her fury. Inside her apron pocket, folded and secret, lies the deed to this entire empire.

scenes.caption: Clean it. Now.
scenes.narration: In the dining hall, the arrogant heir Julian Ashford flicks a wine glass to the floor, shattering it. 'Clean it,' he sneers, stepping over the shards.

scenes.caption: You took everything from my family.
scenes.narration: On her knees among broken glass, Elena's memory flashes to her father, ruined and destroyed by these very people. Her jaw tightens with silent vengeance.

scenes.caption: All of this is legally mine.
scenes.narration: Later, alone in the study, Elena slips the deed from her apron and studies the Ashford signatures. Every room, every jewel, every breath they take belongs to her.

scenes.caption: What is that in your hands?
scenes.narration: Suddenly the study door swings open. Victoria stands frozen in the doorway, her eyes locked on the document in Elena's hands.

scenes.caption: Give me that paper... or else.
scenes.narration: Victoria's voice drops to ice. 'Give me that paper — or I'll destroy you the way I destroyed everyone else.' Elena's grip tightens. The war has only just begun.

episodes.title: The Photograph They Should Have Burned

scenes.caption: Some secrets should have been burned.
scenes.narration: They think I am nothing. A servant polishing silver for the family that destroyed mine. But tonight, dusting the forgotten study, my fingers freeze on a drawer they should have locked.

scenes.caption: My mother... and their patriarch.
scenes.narration: An old photograph, edges yellowed by twenty years. My mother, young and smiling, standing beside a man in an expensive suit. The man who signed away everything she owned.

scenes.caption: He built his empire on her ruin.
scenes.narration: Footsteps. Richard Whitmore fills the doorway, silver-haired and cold, the man who buried my family in lies. He does not yet know the maid holds his deepest sin.

scenes.caption: Play the servant. For now.
scenes.narration: "What are you doing in here?" he snaps. I lower my eyes like the obedient servant they believe I am. But inside, a fire twenty years old roars back to life.

scenes.caption: He fears the truth more than death.
scenes.narration: He tears the room apart searching for the photo, never guessing it now sits against my heart. "If anyone finds that picture," he mutters, "I am finished."

scenes.caption: She left me a message.
scenes.narration: That night, in my tiny servant's room, I turn the photograph over. On the back, in my mother's handwriting, three words that change everything.

scenes.caption: My enemy... is my father.
scenes.narration: "He is your father." The words blur through my tears. The tyrant I swore to destroy — is the man whose blood runs in my veins.

scenes.caption: She knew all along.
scenes.narration: Suddenly my door creaks open. Margaret Whitmore stands there, smiling like a viper. "So," she whispers, "the little maid finally knows the truth."

episodes.title: A Toast to the Woman They Wronged

scenes.caption: They toasted their empire. They never knew it was mine.
scenes.narration: The Ashfords raise their glasses to celebrate the empire they believe is theirs. They toast their triumph, never dreaming the woman refilling their crystal owns every brick beneath their feet.

scenes.caption: 'To the family that built it all.'
scenes.narration: "To the family that built it all," Camilla declares, her voice dripping with false grace. Elena lowers her eyes, a private smile hidden behind years of silence.

scenes.caption: He snapped his fingers. He'd regret it soon.
scenes.narration: Julian snaps his fingers, ordering more wine, treating her like furniture. He has no idea the deed she carries could erase his name by morning.

scenes.caption: The ring they thought was lost forever.
scenes.narration: As Elena pours, her sleeve slips, revealing a signet ring the Ashfords once thought lost forever. Camilla's glass freezes at her lips.

scenes.caption: 'Where did you get that ring?'
scenes.narration: "Where did you get that ring?" Camilla hisses, the room falling silent. Elena straightens her spine, no longer the servant they bought.

scenes.caption: 'A toast to the woman you wronged.'
scenes.narration: Elena sets down the bottle and lifts an empty glass of her own. "Allow me," she says, "to make a toast to the woman you wronged."

scenes.caption: 'All of it is mine.'
scenes.narration: From her apron she draws a folded document stamped in gold. "This mansion, this empire, this name — all of it is mine." Victor's glass shatters on the marble.

scenes.caption: The man they buried just walked in.
scenes.narration: But as the family reels, the great doors swing open — and a man they buried years ago walks in, calling Elena's name. She turns, and the color drains from her face.

scenes.caption: Midnight. The study they forbade her to enter.
scenes.narration: The mansion sleeps, but Elena does not. Tonight, the locked study finally surrenders its secrets to the woman they call a servant.

scenes.caption: One turn of the key changes everything.
scenes.narration: The key turns. The door groans open. Every answer she has bled for waits inside this room.

scenes.caption: The proof was here all along.
scenes.narration: On the desk, a leather folder bearing the Ashford crest — the same family that stole her father's empire.

scenes.caption: They forged his signature. They took it all.
scenes.narration: Contracts. Forged signatures. The murder of her family's fortune written in ink. Her hands tremble with fury.

scenes.caption: The maid holds the deed to their empire.
scenes.narration: She slips the deed to the entire estate from her hidden pocket. She truly owns them all. They just don't know yet.

scenes.caption: Footsteps. She is not alone.
scenes.narration: Suddenly — footsteps in the corridor. The lamp behind her flickers. Someone knows she is here.

scenes.caption: "Cleaning at midnight, my dear?"
scenes.narration: The door swings wide. Victor Ashford, master of the house, stands in his silk robe — a cruel smile curling his lips.

scenes.caption: He sees the deed. The mask shatters.
scenes.narration: His eyes drop to the deed in her hand. His smile dies. "Where," he whispers, "did you get that?" The war has begun.

episodes.title: The Signature That Buys a Dynasty

scenes.caption: One signature. One dynasty. One reckoning.
scenes.narration: Dawn breaks over the Hartwell estate, but Elena has not slept. In her trembling hands rests the document that will end them all.

scenes.caption: The old lion thinks he's already won.
scenes.narration: Victor Hartwell summons the family to sign away the last shares of the empire, certain of his triumph.

scenes.caption: He never noticed the servant who owns him.
scenes.narration: Marcus watches the maid enter, unaware the woman he mocked now holds his family's fate.

scenes.caption: This estate was never yours to sell.
scenes.narration: Elena steps forward and lays the deed on the table, its faded signature glowing under the chandeliers.

scenes.caption: The signature he prayed no one would find.
scenes.narration: Victor's face drains of color as he recognizes his own father's handwriting, the truth he buried decades ago.

scenes.caption: The maid was always the master.
scenes.narration: 'Every floor I scrubbed,' Elena whispers, 'I was already standing in my own house.' The family recoils.

scenes.caption: He has a signature of his own.
scenes.narration: But as Victor reaches into his jacket, a second document appears — one Elena has never seen. His smile returns, colder than before.

episodes.title: She Called Me Family—Then Sold Me Out

scenes.caption: She called me family.
scenes.narration: For weeks the Ashford matriarch treated me like a daughter. But daughters are not sold to the highest bidder.

scenes.caption: A 'gift' with a hidden knife.
scenes.narration: That morning, Vivian pressed papers into my hands, calling it a gift. I smiled—until I read the fine print.

scenes.caption: Sold. Like property.
scenes.narration: She had signed me away—leased my labor, my silence, my life—to a rival tycoon, like furniture.

scenes.caption: 'You were only an asset.'
scenes.narration: 'You were never one of us,' Vivian hissed. 'You were an asset. And assets get traded.'

scenes.caption: They forgot who owns it all.
scenes.narration: They forgot one thing. Every deed in this house—every stone, every acre—already belongs to me.

scenes.caption: The real papers surface.
scenes.narration: That night I returned to the locked study, unlocked the safe, and laid the true documents on the table.

scenes.caption: Let the trap close.
scenes.narration: When Vivian summoned her lawyers to finalize my 'sale,' I let them enter—then locked the doors behind them.

scenes.caption: The dead walk back in.
scenes.narration: I slid the deed across the table. 'You didn't sell me,' I whispered. 'You just signed everything to the woman you wronged.' Then the doors flew open—and someone stepped in who was supposed to be dead.

episodes.title: The Boardroom Knows My Real Name

scenes.caption: They think I'm just the maid.
scenes.narration: They summoned me to the boardroom to serve coffee, not knowing today the servant becomes the sovereign.

scenes.caption: He built this empire on my family's grave.
scenes.narration: Gerald Ashford, the patriarch who once called me hired help, smirks as he opens the merger papers.

scenes.caption: 'The controlling shareholder is present.'
scenes.narration: Then the lawyer clears his throat and reads a name that turns every face to stone.

scenes.caption: Elena Vasquez. The rightful owner.
scenes.narration: I set down the tray, remove my apron, and speak the name they buried long ago—my name.

scenes.caption: He finally remembers the girl he ruined.
scenes.narration: Gerald's coffee cup shatters on the marble floor as recognition drains the color from his face.

scenes.caption: You've been working for me all along.
scenes.narration: I slide the deed across the polished table—every asset, every share, signed to me.

scenes.caption: My sister. Alive. And on his side.
scenes.narration: But then the boardroom doors burst open, and a woman I thought long dead walks in smiling.

scenes.caption: Two heirs. One throne. War begins.
scenes.narration: She holds up a second deed—identical to mine—and asks which of us the empire truly obeys.

episodes.title: Everything You Own Was Always Mine

scenes.caption: You don't fire the woman who owns your house.
scenes.narration: They gathered in the marble foyer expecting to fire the maid. Instead, they found the woman who owns the walls they're standing on.

scenes.caption: He thinks I'm bluffing.
scenes.narration: Victor laughs, sure this is some pathetic bluff. He has no idea the deed left his family's hands years ago.

scenes.caption: Read it. Then read it again.
scenes.narration: I slide the folder across the table. Every property, every account, every lie—signed over to me.

scenes.caption: The maid was never the maid.
scenes.narration: Vivienne's face drains white. The woman she called family, then sold out, holds their entire dynasty in her palm.

scenes.caption: Everything you own was always mine.
scenes.narration: Julian lunges, snarling that I'm nothing but hired help. I remind him the mortgage on his very bed is mine.

scenes.caption: I don't need your key anymore.
scenes.narration: I take the master key from my pocket—the same one they gave the maid—and drop it at their feet.

scenes.caption: He whispered a name that stopped my heart.
scenes.narration: But as security arrives to escort them out, Victor smiles—and whispers that he still has one card left. The daughter I thought was dead.

scenes.caption: They called for the maid. A queen answered.
scenes.narration: They summoned me to the grand hall one last time, expecting a servant to grovel. Instead, I walked in wearing the empire I now own.

scenes.caption: Everything she protected belonged to me.
scenes.narration: Vivian, the matriarch who once made me scrub her floors, clutched her pearls in disbelief. The throne she guarded was never truly hers.

scenes.caption: The deed to everything you love.
scenes.narration: Gerald slammed his fist on the antique table, demanding I explain myself. I simply slid the deed across the polished wood.

scenes.caption: You taught me betrayal. I mastered it.
scenes.narration: Marcus, the son who once smiled while betraying me, begged me to remember what we were. I remembered everything—that was the problem.

scenes.caption: The photo they should have destroyed.
scenes.narration: I placed the old photograph on the mantel—the one they should have burned. Proof I was the rightful heir all along.

scenes.caption: The maid takes her throne.
scenes.narration: One by one, the staff who once ignored me now bowed. The empire's true owner had finally claimed her throne.

scenes.caption: 'The real heir is still alive.'
scenes.narration: But as I savored my victory, my phone buzzed with a message that froze my blood: 'The real heir is still alive.' Someone else wants this empire too.
